reasoning: The Lean signature quantifies over all integers `x` and all primes `p : ℕ`, assumes `(p : ℤ) ∣ (x^4 - x^2 + 1)`, and concludes `(p : ℤ) ≡ 1 [ZMOD 12]`, which unfolds to `p % 12 = 1` — exactly "p ≡ 1 mod 12". This matches the informal "any prime divisor of x^4 - x^2 + 1 is congruent to 1 modulo 12" directly. Since x^4 - x^2 + 1 is always odd (x²(x²-1) is even) and always ≡ 1 mod 3, the primes 2 and 3 never appear as divisors, so no extra hypotheses are needed. The quantifier structure, hypotheses, types (ℕ prime, ℤ divisibility), and conclusion all line up with the informal statement.
